title: Detection of multiple drivers for a signal
body:
I am using  Verilator 5.027 devel rev v5.026-110-g18fc3e608 (from oss-cad-suite) and have the following code that drives the *driven* signal from two always blocks and I would like to get a warning when this is happening.

```
module badcode ();

  reg clk;

  initial begin
    clk = 0;
    forever begin
      #5 clk = ~clk;
    end
  end

  initial begin
    #30 $finish();
  end


  reg driven;

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    driven <= 1;
  end

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    driven <= 0;
  end

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    $display("Bad code is %x", driven);
  end
endmodule
```

Compilation 
```
 verilator --binary  --timing --Wall badcode.v
make: Entering directory '/home/keesj/tmp/badcode/obj_dir'
g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -fcoroutines -c -o verilated.o /home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/verilated.cpp
g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -fcoroutines -c -o verilated_timing.o /home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp
g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -fcoroutines -c -o verilated_threads.o /home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/verilated_threads.cpp
python3 /home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/bin/verilator_includer -DVL_INCLUDE_OPT=include Vbadcode.cpp Vbadcode___024root__DepSet_h2687ef9c__0.cpp Vbadcode___024root__DepSet_h54c1449a__0.cpp Vbadcode__main.cpp Vbadcode___024root__Slow.cpp Vbadcode___024root__DepSet_h54c1449a__0__Slow.cpp Vbadcode__Syms.cpp > Vbadcode__ALL.cpp
g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include -I/home/keesj/opt/oss-cad-suite/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -fcoroutines -c -o Vbadcode__ALL.o Vbadcode__ALL.cpp
echo "" > Vbadcode__ALL.verilator_deplist.tmp
g++    verilated.o verilated_timing.o verilated_threads.o Vbadcode__ALL.a    -pthread -lpthread -latomic   -o Vbadcode
rm Vbadcode__ALL.verilator_deplist.tmp
make: Leaving directory '/home/keesj/tmp/badcode/obj_dir'
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.027 devel rev v5.026-110-g18fc3e608
- Verilator: Built from 0.014 MB sources in 2 modules, into 0.023 MB in 7 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 4.288 s (elab=0.000, cvt=0.001, bld=4.285); cpu 0.003 s on 1 threads; alloced 1.410 MB
```

Running 
```
$ ./obj_dir/Vbadcode
Bad code is 0
Bad code is 0
Bad code is 0
- badcode.v:13: Verilog $finish
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.027 devel
- Verilator: $finish at 35ps; walltime 0.001 s; speed 15.846 ns/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.002 s on 1 threads; alloced 168 MB
```

Note that I tried 3 other tools(verible-verilog-lint, svlint and iverilog) that also did not appear to detect this.
```
verible-verilog-lint badcode.v
```
```
iverilog badcode.v
./a.out
Bad code is x
Bad code is 1
Bad code is 0
badcode.v:13: $finish called at 30 (1s)
```
